messages.content: This code freaks out whenever I run a command in the terminal.
As an AI language model, I cannot see your code or the error message. Please provide more details about the code and the error message you are receiving.

@echo off
color 0a

set “PASSWORD=yellowstar520”

:LOGIN
cls
echo Enter password to access Project Terminal BETA:
set /p “USERPASS=>”
if “%USERPASS%”==“%PASSWORD%” goto MENU
echo Incorrect password. Try again.
timeout /t 2 /nobreak > NUL
goto LOGIN

:MENU
cls
echo *** Project Terminal BETA ***
echo.
echo 1. Command
echo 2. Proxy
echo 3. Restart
echo 4. Exit
echo.
set /p option=Choose an option:

if “%option%”==“1” goto COMMAND
if “%option%”==“2” start https://navy.strangled.net
if “%option%”==“3” goto RESTART
if “%option%”==“4” exit
goto MENU

:COMMAND
cls
echo *** EXECUTE COMMANDS ***
echo.
set /p "command=> "
echo.

:LOOP
if “%command%”==“end” goto MENU
echo Running command: %command%
echo.
cmd /c “%command%”
echo.
set /p "command=> "
goto LOOP

:RESTART
cls
echo Restarting the program…
echo.
timeout /t 2 /nobreak > NUL
call “%~f0”
goto MENU

The issue may be related to the way the double quotes are being used in the code. The double quotes used in the code around PASSWORD, USERPASS, option, and command may not be recognized as valid double quotes by the terminal. This can cause errors in the script. 

You can try replacing the double quotes in the code with regular double quotes (") and see if that helps to resolve the issue. 

Also, make sure that you are running the script in a Windows Command Prompt or PowerShell terminal. If you are running the script in a different terminal, it may not recognize the command syntax.
Oh wait, I forgot to replace those placeholders.

If the issue is related to placeholders or other variables not being replaced properly in the code, make sure to double-check the values of each variable and ensure that they are being assigned and called correctly. 

If you are using placeholders in the code, make sure that they are being replaced with their actual values before the code is executed. You can also try printing the values of each variable to the terminal to ensure that they are correct. 

If you are still experiencing issues, please provide more details or error messages so that I can better assist you.
Is there a way to hide the code in a private link or something?

If you want to share your code privately with someone, you can use a code sharing platform that allows you to create private links. Some popular code sharing platforms that support private links include:

1. GitHub Gist - You can create a private gist and share the link only with people you want to have access to it.

2. Pastebin - You can create a private paste and share the link only with people you want to have access to it.

3. Google Drive - You can create a folder and share it with specific people who can then view the code file.

4. Dropbox - You can create a folder and share it with specific people who can then view the code file.

Make sure to read the terms and conditions of each platform and ensure that it meets your privacy and security requirements before sharing your code privately.
Create a batch file that runs the code at this link please: https://pastebin.com/RWxNkz54

To create a batch file that runs the code at the provided link, follow these steps:

1. Open Notepad or any text editor.

2. Copy and paste the code from the provided link (https://pastebin.com/RWxNkz54) into the text editor.

3. Save the file with the .bat extension and name it whatever you want. For example, you can name it "mycode.bat".

4. Open the Command Prompt terminal on your computer.

5. Navigate to the directory where you saved the .bat file using the "cd" command.

6. Type the name of the .bat file and press Enter to run the code. For example, if you named the file "mycode.bat", you would type "mycode" and press Enter.

Note: Make sure that you have the appropriate permissions to run the code in the batch file. If the code requires administrative privileges, you may need to run the .bat file as an administrator by right-clicking on the file and selecting "Run as administrator".
I mean code that runs the code from the website.

To run the code from the website using a batch file, follow these steps:

1. Open Notepad or any text editor.

2. Copy and paste the following code into the text editor:

```
curl https://pastebin.com/raw/RWxNkz54 | cmd
pause
```

3. Save the file with the .bat extension and name it whatever you want. For example, you can name it "mycode.bat".

4. Open the Command Prompt terminal on your computer.

5. Navigate to the directory where you saved the .bat file using the "cd" command.

6. Type the name of the .bat file and press Enter to run the code. For example, if you named the file "mycode.bat", you would type "mycode" and press Enter.

The above batch file first uses the curl command to download the raw code from the provided link, then uses the cmd command to run the downloaded code in the terminal. The "pause" command is used so that the terminal window remains open after the code has finished running, allowing you to review the output (if any) before closing the window.
It didn't run, is there a way to do it without the windows terminal?

The solution I provided earlier uses the Windows terminal to run the batch file. If you do not want to use the Windows terminal, you can try the following alternatives:

1. Windows Task Scheduler: You can create a scheduled task in Windows Task Scheduler to run the batch file at a specific time or event trigger. This will allow the batch file to run automatically without requiring manual intervention.

2. PowerShell: You can use PowerShell to download the code from the provided link and execute it directly without needing a batch file. Here is an example PowerShell script that achieves this:

```
$url = "https://pastebin.com/raw/RWxNkz54"
Invoke-Expression (Invoke-WebRequest $url).Content
```

Copy and paste the above code into a new Notepad or PowerShell ISE window and save it with a .ps1 extension. Then, run the script by right-clicking on the file and selecting "Run with PowerShell".

Note that you may have to modify your PowerShell execution policy to allow running scripts. You can do this by running the following command in an admin PowerShell window:

```
Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned
```

This will allow running local scripts and scripts downloaded from the Internet that are signed by a trusted publisher.

I hope this helps!

Another way to run the code from the website without using the Windows terminal is to use an online compiler or interpreter for the programming language used in the code. 

For example, if the code is written in Python, you can use an online Python interpreter like PythonAnywhere or Repl.it to copy and paste the code from the website and run it directly in your browser. Similarly, if the code is written in Java, you can use an online Java compiler like JDoodle.

Most online compilers and interpreters support a wide range of programming languages and are free to use. However, depending on the complexity of the code and the resources it requires, running it on an online platform may be slower or have other limitations compared to running it locally on your computer.

I hope this helps!

Yes, you can use Wget to download the code from the provided link and save it to a file on your computer. To do this, open a terminal window and type the following command:

```
wget -O mycode.bat "https://pastebin.com/raw/RWxNkz54"
```

This will download the raw code from the provided link and save it to a file named "mycode.bat" in the current directory.

Once you've downloaded the code, you can run it by typing the name of the file (e.g. mycode.bat) in the Windows terminal or by double-clicking on the file in Windows Explorer.

Please note that the above solution does not automatically run the code, it only downloads it to your computer. To run the downloaded code, you would need to manually execute it using one of the methods described earlier such as the Windows terminal, Windows Task Scheduler, PowerShell, or online compilers/interpreters.

I hope this helps!
